behavior_summary: Responds to a plate/item being hit: sets the item's visual frame to the "pressed/active" frame (frame=0x01) unless it is already in that frame, decodes the item's packed quality word into a low byte (type/index) and a high byte (flags/metadata), and then selects behavior based on that decoded type/flags.

For specific plate types (low byte values like 0x01 or 0x02 inferred), runs a coordinated multi-plate ("hanoi") flow: it checks a global busy guard (hanoiBusy), optionally plays a blocked sound, sets hanoiBusy to prevent reentry, searches nearby plates/items for a best candidate (distance-based selection using X/Y deltas), records candidate footpad metadata and stores the chosen candidate identifier into the global hanoiStairDown, and then invokes an internal helper (U8P_PLATE::func0411) to carry out the stair/slider action.

For slider-like plates (inferred by flags in the high byte), will trigger spawn calls to SLIDER::activateSlider or SLIDER::deactivateSlider on matching items (quality-equality checks) rather than the multi-plate flow.
Provides a 'U8P_PLATE::release' process (U8P_PLATE::release) that resets the pressed frame to 0x00 when no surface weight is present, effectively clearing the pressed visual state.
Uses sound (playSFX) for blocked/invalid activations and uses suspend in several paths to wait for spawned activities or to pause the process while external actions complete.
notable_control_flow: Early-exit guard: U8P_PLATE::gotHit skips heavy work if Item::getFrame(this) == 0x01 (already pressed).
Type-dispatch: U8P_PLATE::gotHit splits Item::getQuality(this) into low and high bytes and branches behavior by the low byte (type/index) and selective tests on high-byte flags.
Hanoi coordination: if the low-byte indicates a multi-plate action, code checks global hanoiBusy (read) and sets it (write) before selecting a candidate; blocked path plays SFX instead of starting.

Spatial candidate selection: iterates nearby items, computes delta X/Y (Item::getX/Y) and filters by X-range, tracks minimum Y distance to pick the best candidate, and calls Item::getFootpad on the winner to store footpad outputs.

Action handoff: after candidate selection, U8P_PLATE::gotHit calls U8P_PLATE::func0411 which performs spawn of SLIDER::activateSlider/deactivateSlider or Item::setQuality updates, may call AccumulateIntelligence, and clears hanoiBusy at the end. Some branches in U8P_PLATE::func0411 use suspend.
Release path: U8P_PLATE::release clears frame to 0x00 only when Item::getFrame(this)==0x01 and Item::getSurfaceWeight(this)==0x00.
open_questions: Exact encoding of the quality word: what each bit/byte in Item::getQuality represents (precise meaning of low-byte values like 0x01/0x02 and each high-byte flag). (confidence: low)
Meaning and origin of param values passed into U8P_PLATE::func0411 (examples: constant 0x029A and how param1 is used as both selector and flag). (confidence: medium)
Precise semantics of hanoiStairDown stored value: whether it is an item id, an index, or footpad index used elsewhere and how other systems consume it. (confidence: medium)
Whether some spawn/suspend interactions can deadlock or require external cleanup (timing/suspend semantics across spawned SLIDER:: routines). (confidence: low)

main_function.name: U8P_PLATE::gotHit
main_function.purpose: Handle activation when the plate/item is hit: set visual state, decode item type/flags, coordinate either a multi-plate 'hanoi' action or slider activation/deactivation with nearby plates, play feedback SFX for blocked activations, and hand off execution to U8P_PLATE::func0411 to perform the concrete action (which may spawn slider routines and suspend the process).
entry_conditions: Invoked when this plate receives a 'U8P_PLATE::gotHit' event.
Performs main behavior only if Item::getFrame(this) != 0x01 (i.e., not already pressed).

function_map.name: U8P_PLATE::gotHit
function_map.role: Item activation handler that implements press logic, candidate selection, and orchestrates multi-plate or slider actions.

function_map.name: U8P_PLATE::release
function_map.role: Process that clears the pressed visual frame (Item::setFrame(0x00)) when there is no surface weight; used to 'U8P_PLATE::release' the plate's visual state.

function_map.name: U8P_PLATE::func0411
function_map.role: Performs the concrete follow-up action after candidate selection: spawns slider activation/deactivation, updates item quality when appropriate, clears the hanoiBusy guard, and performs bookkeeping (AccumulateIntelligence). May suspend the current process in some branches.

internal_calls.purpose: Handoff to a helper that carries out slider/stair activation, quality updates, clears hanoiBusy, and may suspend. Called once a candidate and control flags are prepared.

external_calls.purpose: Check current visual frame to avoid double-activating an already pressed plate.
Set the plate to pressed/active visual frame (0x01) on activation.
Read a packed quality word used to determine plate type (low byte) and flags/metadata (high byte) to branch activation behavior.
Play feedback sound when activation is blocked (e.g., hanoiBusy) or invalid.
Obtain X coordinate for distance/range filtering during nearby-item candidate selection.
Obtain Y coordinate for vertical-distance comparison when selecting the closest candidate.
Retrieve footpad/placement metadata (multiple outs) from a chosen candidate and store outputs for later use.
Spawn the slider activation routine on a matched item when its quality indicates activation (used to drive slide/down behavior).
Spawn the slider deactivation routine on a matched item when quality/flags indicate deactivation.
Re-check a referenced item's quality to decide whether to call Item::setQuality or to spawn slider activation.
Update a referenced item's quality using the selector param when conditions (low byte == 0x01 or 0x02) are met.
Bookkeeping/AI-intel accumulation after certain activation paths (observed in a param-specific branch).
Suspend the current process in some branches, typically after spawning or when waiting for external state changes.
Check whether any surface weight remains before clearing the pressed frame; ensure U8P_PLATE::release only when weight==0.

reads.name: hanoiBusy
reads.likely_effect: Gates whether a new multi-plate/hanoi action can start; reading it prevents starting when already busy (blocks with SFX).

reads.name: hanoiStairBlocked
reads.likely_effect: Used by U8P_PLATE::func0411 to decide whether to remap or block an action (e.g., when param1==0x01).

writes.name: hanoiBusy
writes.likely_effect: Set to true when beginning a hanoi/multi-plate activation and cleared later by U8P_PLATE::func0411 to prevent concurrent activations.

writes.name: hanoiStairDown
writes.likely_effect: Written with the chosen candidate identifier/index so downstream logic (or U8P_PLATE::func0411) knows which stair/plate to drive down.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Responds to a plate/item being hit: sets the item's visual frame to the "pressed/active" frame (frame=0x01) unless it is already in that frame, decodes the item's packed quality word into a low byte (type/index) and a high byte (flags/metadata), and then selects behavior based on that decoded type/flags.
